name: Monasterio de Tentudía – circular by the Ruta de la Pantanera
name_original: Monasterio de Tentudía – circular por la Ruta de la Pantanera
region_intro: Hiking around Calera De León offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Tentudía in Extremadura, Spain. The region features varied ecosystems, transitioning from olive and holm oak groves at lower elevations to oak, chestnut, and pine forests higher up. Hikers can explore ancient dehesa landscapes and sections of historic routes, including the Vía de la Plata. The terrain includes hills and the highest point in the province of Badajoz, Cerro de Tentudía, at 1,112 meters. Best hiking trails around Calera De León The most popular hiking route is Monasterio de Tentudía – circular by the Ruta de la Pantanera, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate circular path explores the area around the historic Monasterio de Tentudía. Another top favourite among local hikers is Calera de León Dehesa and Vía de la Plata Loop, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This route allows exploration of ancient dehesa landscapes and sections of the Roman Vía de la Plata. Local hikers also love the Pico Bonales via the Collado de la Majada del Moral, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through varied forest and hill terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Calera De León is defined by dehesa landscapes, oak and pine forests, and historic routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in Calera De León are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Calera De León's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Calera de León?
A: Calera de León offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find olive and holm oak groves at lower elevations, transitioning to oak, chestnut, and pine forests as you ascend into the Sierra de Tentudía. The region is also characterized by ancient dehesa landscapes, providing a rich variety of natural scenery.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Calera de León?
A: The trails around Calera de León cater to various fitness levels. Out of 27 available routes, 5 are considered easy, 20 are moderate, and 2 are difficult. This means there are plenty of options for both casual walkers and more experienced hikers seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or interesting sights along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical and cultural points of interest. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa along the Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, visit the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4093491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Tentudía&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3518848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;. Ethnographic treasures like the 'fuente de la Malena' (Malena's fountain) and ancient snow wells can also be found.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Calera de León?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991569389/monasterio-de-tentudia-circular-by-the-ruta-de-la-pantanera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Tentudía – circular by the Ruta de la Pantanera&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.6-mile (9.0 km) loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991710605/calera-de-leon-dehesa-and-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calera de León Dehesa and Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.7-mile (12.4 km) path.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Calera de León?
A: The hiking trails in Calera de León are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse dehesa landscapes, the peaceful oak and pine forests, and the historical routes that offer a blend of nature and culture.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to high points with panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991574410/pico-bonales-via-the-collado-de-la-majada-del-moral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Bonales via the Collado de la Majada del Moral&lt;/a&gt; trail leads to an area offering great views. Additionally, the Monasterio de Tentudía itself is located at Cerro de Tentudía, the highest point in the province of Badajoz, providing stunning panoramic vistas of the surrounding region.

Q: Can I hike sections of the historic Vía de la Plata in Calera de León?
A: Yes, the Vía de la Plata, a historic route, crosses the beautiful Dehesa de Calera de León. You can explore sections of this ancient path, such as on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991710605/calera-de-leon-dehesa-and-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calera de León Dehesa and Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers picturesque views of the Extremaduran interior.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Calera de León?
A: The region's scenery transforms with the changing seasons, offering unique experiences year-round. However, spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for hiking, with pleasant weather and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be hot, while winters are milder but can have cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the region offers 5 easy trails that would be suitable for families. These shorter, less strenuous routes allow for enjoyable outdoor experiences without significant challenges. Examples of local trails mentioned in the region research include 'Embalse de Tentudía' (11.66 km, easy, circular) and 'Los Barrancos' (5.21 km, easy, circular).

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Calera de León?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed, Calera de León is a village with infrastructure to support visitors. It is generally possible to find parking in or near the village, especially for routes that start close to the town center or major landmarks like the Monasterio de Tentudía.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike in Calera de León?
A: The highest point you can reach is Cerro de Tentudía, at 1,112 meters above sea level. This is where the Monasterio de Tentudía is located, offering not only a significant cultural landmark but also breathtaking panoramic views from the summit.

Q: Are there opportunities for stargazing in Calera de León?
A: Yes, Calera de León is celebrated for its dark skies, making it an exceptional location for stargazing. After a day of hiking, the clear night skies offer a unique natural spectacle, adding another dimension to your outdoor experience in the region.
tips: The legend about the Tentudía Monastery has its origin in the 13th century, during the Reconquest, when the monarch Fernando III El Santo commissioned the master Pelay Pérez Correa, of the Order of Santiago, to take the mountains of the Tentudía region from the Saracens. .
